commit.message:
fix: redesign tree navigation to VS Code / macOS Finder model

The old model had three unintuitive behaviors:
1. Down() auto-expanded command nodes and section headers as you
   navigated past them — you couldn't browse without accidental expansion.
2. Left() collapsed a node AND jumped to the parent in one step —
   collapsing then navigating to a sibling required an extra Down pass
   back through the parent to reach the sibling.
3. Shift+Left/Right operated on the entire tree, not the current subtree.
4. Vim mode: lowercase 'h' was claimed by the help toggle, leaving vim
   users without a Left key.

New behavior (matches VS Code / macOS Finder / IntelliJ tree navigation):

  ↓ / j — move to next visible row; never auto-expand anything
  ↑ / k — move to previous visible row (unchanged)
  → / l  — collapsed node: expand and STAY on it (first press)
            expanded node: jump to first command child (second press)
            flag/positional: no-op
  ← / h  — expanded node: collapse and STAY on it
            collapsed node or leaf: go to parent
            flag/positional: jump to owner command row

  Shift+→ — expand current node's subtree (not global);
             at root = expand all
  Shift+← — collapse current node's subtree, stay on node;
             at root = collapse all

This makes 'collapse then navigate to sibling' natural:
  ← collapses the node and stays; ↓ reaches the next sibling.

Additional changes:
- Vim mode 'h' now correctly maps to Left (help toggle is H/ctrl+p only)
- Left() uses depth-based parent fallback when FullPath is absent
- Added IsAtRoot(), CollapseSubtree() to TreeModel
- Added tests: Right expands-and-stays, Left collapse-and-stay, Left
  parent fallback, Shift subtree scope, Down no-auto-expand
- Updated status bar hint text to reflect new ←/→ semantics

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Build Check gate running in parallel with Implementer

The Build Check (gate) and Lint Fix (deterministic) steps both have
role: .implementer, causing them to be included in the parallel
implementer batch. This meant Build Check would test code before the
Implementer finished writing it — ~66% chain failure rate.

Fix: Filter implementerIndices by both role AND stepType.isAgentic
so only actual LLM-driven agents participate in parallel execution.
Gate and deterministic steps now correctly run sequentially after
all agentic implementers complete.

Affected locations:
- applyPlannerOverrides: preserves gate/deterministic in postAgents
- runAgentsWithParallelImplementers: 3 filter sites + hasGaps check
- StepType: add isAgentic computed property

commit.message:
doc: add gateway application API specification (#6)

* doc: add gateway application API specification

Defines the API surface for external applications to interact with
the Sonde gateway. The gateway is a platform service; application
logic runs in a separate process communicating over a local socket.

API covers:
- Transport (Unix socket / named pipe, length-prefixed CBOR)
- 10 request types (subscribe, assign program, set schedule, etc.)
- 3 event types (APP_DATA_EVENT, NODE_EVENT, RESPONSE)
- Node identity model (HMAC key as primary identifier)
- Error handling, concurrency model, lifecycle
- 3 open questions (transport choice, multi-subscriber, key exposure)

Also adds cross-references from README and gateway-requirements.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: rewrite gateway API as developer-focused application API

Split concerns:
- Application API (this doc): minimal data-plane for developers
  - 4 message types: DATA, DATA_REPLY, EVENT, LOG
  - Opaque node_id (no keys/hints exposed)
  - Two handler models: process-per-message and streaming
  - Developer never sees protocol, auth, or node management
- Gateway admin (gateway-requirements.md): ops/config concerns
  - Node provisioning, program deployment, scheduling
  - Monitoring, diagnostics, failover

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: unify handler models — stdin/stdout with auto-respawn

Single transport model: gateway spawns handler, streams via
stdin/stdout with length-prefixed CBOR. If handler exits, gateway
respawns on next message. If handler stays alive, gateway keeps
streaming. Developer doesn't choose — the gateway adapts.

Resolves A-1 (spawn cost) and A-2 (connection direction).
Includes both one-shot and long-running Python examples.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: resolve A-1 — program_hash to handler routing

Handlers are mapped by program_hash. Admin chooses granularity:
one handler per program, one handler for many programs, or a
catch-all. Unmatched programs get a zero-length reply.

All open questions resolved.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: split send into send (fire-and-forget) and send_recv (request-response)

BPF program controls whether it expects a reply:
- send(ptr, len): fire-and-forget, no APP_DATA_REPLY
- send_recv(ptr, len, reply_buf, reply_len, timeout_ms):
  blocks until APP_DATA_REPLY or timeout

APP_DATA gains reply_expected and timeout_ms fields.
APP_DATA_REPLY only sent when reply_expected is true.
Timeout is BPF-controlled, not gateway/handler config.

Updated across all three docs:
- README: helper API, application data section
- protocol.md: CBOR keys, APP_DATA/APP_DATA_REPLY, flow diagrams
- gateway-api.md: DATA/DATA_REPLY messages, config (no timeout_s)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: remove reply_expected flag — handler reply length is implicit

The BPF program and handler are written by the same developer.
They agree a priori on which messages expect replies. No flag
needed on the wire:

- Handler replies with data → gateway sends APP_DATA_REPLY
- Handler replies with zero-length → gateway sends nothing
- Node: send() doesn't wait, send_recv() waits with timeout

Gateway-handler link is always half-duplex (DATA → DATA_REPLY).
Node-gateway radio link is simplex or half-duplex depending on
which BPF helper was called.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: mention send_recv() alongside send() in overview

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* doc: address PR #6 review comments

README.md:
- Remove reply_expected=true reference from send_recv() description

protocol.md:
- Replace 'only if reply_expected' with 'only if handler replies
  with data' in all flow diagrams (§6.1, §6.2, §6.3, §6.5)

gateway-api.md:
- Fix 'next DATA' to 'next message' for respawn trigger
- Add note that msg_type values are specific to handler API,
  unrelated to node-gateway protocol msg_types
- Add 1 MB max message size and CBOR integer key clarification
- Fix Python examples to use read_exact() helper for robust
  partial-read handling on pipes
- Fix no-handler fallback: no APP_DATA_REPLY sent (not zero-length)
- Fix crash behavior: no reply sent, send_recv() will timeout
- Remove stale timeout_s from config example

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix fork auto-discovery and simplify fork picker UI

Bug #1: Auto-discovery was broken when user toggled 'This is a fork' but
left fork URL empty. The onAddProject callback incorrectly set forkValue
to opts.path, making both origin and upstream point to the same repo.

2. Extracts repo name from parsed URL or git remote

says 'Contributing via your fork?' with explanation text showing what

commit.message:
sumcheck: add SumcheckProof::update_claim helper for BDDT claim tracking (#473)

Add a public helper that reconstructs the round polynomial from BDDT
evaluation points [p(0), cubic_coeff, p(-1)] and evaluates it at the
verifier challenge via Horner's method, all in O(1) without allocating.

Works for both degree-3 (cubic) and degree-2 (quadratic, where
cubic_coeff = 0) claims, unifying the two previously separate inline
patterns.

Refactor Nova's own MemorySumcheckInstance and InnerBatchedSumcheckInstance
to use the new helper, removing ~20 lines of duplicated bookkeeping.

commit.message:
[CBE Token Launch] Piecewise Linear Bonding Curve - Fix CBE Genesis Curve Type (#1854)

* feat(api): expose observer metrics via API endpoints (#1788)

Add API endpoints for consensus observer metrics and network health:

**Per-Height Metrics Endpoints:**
- GET /api/v1/observer/height/current - Current height metrics
- GET /api/v1/observer/height/{height} - Specific height metrics
  - round_count, commit_latency_ms, classification, phases

**Network Health Summary:**
- GET /api/v1/observer/network/health - Network-wide anomaly detection
  - stall_rate, average_rounds_per_height
  - partition_indicators (potential_partition_detected, stalled_heights, divergent_heights)
  - validator_anomaly_scores
  - recent_heights summary

**Surprisal Score (Anomaly Detection):**
- GET /api/v1/observer/surprisal/{height} - Anomaly score for height
  - surprisal_score, baseline_score, deviation_factor, classification

**Classification Logic:**
- healthy: 1 round, no stalls
- delayed: 1 round with stall
- recovering: 2-3 rounds, no fault
- degraded: 2-3 rounds with fault
- divergence: 4+ rounds

**Files Changed:**
- zhtp/src/api/handlers/observer.rs (new, 436 lines)
- zhtp/src/api/handlers/mod.rs (+observer module)
- zhtp/src/unified_server.rs (+ObserverHandler registration)

API contracts use stable JSON schemas with versioned structures.

Closes #1788

* fix(observer): normalize URI before route matching

Strip trailing slashes and query strings before dispatching to ensure
requests like /api/v1/observer/network/health?window=20 or with
a trailing slash are properly routed.

Addresses Copilot AI review comment on PR #1806.

* fix(observer): return 501 NotImplemented instead of synthetic data

The observer endpoints were returning synthetic/mock data (height-derived
round counts, hard-coded phase durations, fixed commit latency) instead
of real observer metrics.

Since the ObserverService from lib-consensus is not yet integrated into
the zhtp runtime, the endpoints now return proper HTTP 501 Not Implemented
with a clear message explaining that real observer data is pending.

Changes:
- All four endpoints return 501 NotImplemented
- Removed synthetic data generation helper methods
- Kept classify_height() as documentation of the API contract
- Added TODO comments explaining how to wire to real ObserverService:
  - get_height_analysis(height)
  - get_network_health(window_size)
  - surprisal_stats from HeightAnalysis

Addresses Copilot AI review comment on PR #1806.

* fix(monitor): normalize URI before route matching

Strip trailing slashes and query strings before dispatching to ensure
requests like /api/v1/monitor/health?verbose=1 or with a trailing
slash are properly routed.

Consistent with ObserverHandler and other API handlers.

Addresses Copilot AI review comment on PR #1806.

* Update zhtp/src/api/handlers/observer.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add comprehensive unit tests covering:
- can_handle logic for monitor paths

Note: Full integration tests require RuntimeOrchestrator which is
complex to set up in unit tests. Integration tests should be added

  - BondingCurveBuy: executes buy with slippage protection, persists state

  - POST /api/v1/curve/buy accepts signed BondingCurveBuy tx
  - POST /api/v1/curve/sell accepts signed BondingCurveSell tx

  BondingCurveBuy, BondingCurveSell, BondingCurveGraduate

* feat(pricing): implement Issue #1819 - Unified Token Pricing System (SOV + CBE)

## Summary
Implements unified token pricing system supporting both fixed (SRV) and
dynamic (oracle-derived) pricing modes for SOV and CBE tokens.

## Changes

### Phase 1: TokenPricingState Core
- Add lib-blockchain/src/pricing/mod.rs with unified pricing system
- TokenPricingState tracks CBE/USD oracle price, CBE/SOV ratio, pricing mode
- PricingCalculator for SOV/USD = CBE/USD ÷ CBE/SOV formula
- PricingMode enum: Fixed/Dynamic/PreGraduation/PostGraduation

### Phase 2: Unified Price API
- get_sov_price_unified(): Returns SOV price with mode indicator
  - Dynamic when both oracle signals available
  - Fixed (SRV) fallback when oracle not ready
- get_cbe_price_unified(): Returns CBE price with phase info
- Updated /api/v1/price/{token} endpoint

### Phase 3: Oracle Extension
- Extended OraclePriceAttestation with cbe_usd_price field
- Extended FinalizedOraclePrice with cbe_usd_price field
- Updated aggregation logic for dual-price finalization
- Auto-updates token_pricing_state when CBE price finalized

### Phase 4: Integration Tests
- Add lib-blockchain/tests/unified_pricing_tests.rs
- 8 comprehensive tests covering all functionality

## API Contract
GET /api/v1/price/sov returns:
{
  token_id: sov,
  symbol: SOV,
  price_usd_cents: 500,
  price_mode: dynamic,  // fixed or dynamic
  price_source: oracle_derived,
  confidence_level: high
}

## Backward Compatibility
- All CBE price fields are Option<T> with serde defaults
- Existing attestations without CBE price deserialize as None
- Fixed (SRV) pricing used as fallback

Closes #1819

* feat(pricing): integrate unified pricing across codebase

- Integrate TokenPricingState into Blockchain struct
- Update OraclePriceAttestation with CBE/USD price field
- Update FinalizedOraclePrice with CBE/USD price field
- Update all attestation construction sites
- Update API handlers for unified pricing endpoints
- Update oracle aggregation logic for dual-price finalization

* feat(bonding-curve): implement Issue #1842 - Piecewise Linear Bonding Curve Mathematics

Implements document-compliant piecewise linear curve with 4 supply bands:
- Band 1: [0-10B] slope 2.5e-12, base 0.0003133457
- Band 2: [10B-30B] slope 7.5e-12
- Band 3: [30B-60B] slope 1.5e-11
- Band 4: [60B-100B] slope 3.0e-11

Features:
- Price continuity guaranteed at all band boundaries
- Proper fixed-point arithmetic using 10^16 combined scale
- Supports both positive and negative base offsets

Note: Base values adjusted from spec to ensure continuity while
maintaining exact specification slopes.

The CBE genesis initialization was using a constant-price Linear curve,
but the specification requires the 4-band piecewise linear curve.

Changes:

This ensures CBE follows the document-compliant pricing model from genesis.

commit.message:
Fix workflow artifact upload paths missing "X" in EssentialsX addon jar names (#6462)

### Information

Workflow artifact upload steps referenced incorrect jar filenames — all
addon jars are built as `EssentialsX{Addon}-*.jar` (e.g.
`EssentialsXChat`, `EssentialsXProtect`) because project names in
`settings.gradle.kts` are all prefixed with `EssentialsX`, and
`archivesName` is set to `project.name`. The upload globs were missing
the `X`, causing every per-module direct upload to fail to find its
target file.

### Details

**Proposed fix:**  
Update all per-module `archive: false` upload step paths in both
`build-pr.yml` and `build-master.yml` to use the correct
`EssentialsX`-prefixed names:

| Before | After |
|---|---|
| `jars/EssentialsAntiBuild-*.jar` | `jars/EssentialsXAntiBuild-*.jar` |
| `jars/EssentialsChat-*.jar` | `jars/EssentialsXChat-*.jar` |
| `jars/EssentialsDiscord-*.jar` | `jars/EssentialsXDiscord-*.jar` |
| `jars/EssentialsDiscordLink-*.jar` |
`jars/EssentialsXDiscordLink-*.jar` |
| `jars/EssentialsGeoIP-*.jar` | `jars/EssentialsXGeoIP-*.jar` |
| `jars/EssentialsProtect-*.jar` | `jars/EssentialsXProtect-*.jar` |
| `jars/EssentialsSpawn-*.jar` | `jars/EssentialsXSpawn-*.jar` |
| `jars/EssentialsXMPP-*.jar` | `jars/EssentialsXXMPP-*.jar` |

The Javadoc `cp` paths in `build-master.yml` reference filesystem
directory names (which don't include `X`) and remain correct as-is.

**Environments tested:**

OS: N/A (CI workflow change only)

Java version: N/A

- ~~Most recent Paper version (1.XX.Y, git-Paper-BUILD)~~
- ~~CraftBukkit/Spigot/Paper 1.12.2~~
- ~~CraftBukkit 1.8.8~~

**Demonstration:**

N/A — the incorrect globs would silently produce no artifact (or an
error) for each addon module on every workflow run using `archive:
false`.

commit.message:
feat(shim): block device rootfs delivery with sandbox/container split

Implement the firecracker-containerd pattern for Cloud Hypervisor:
container rootfs delivered as hot-plugged virtio-blk disks.

Sandbox/container detection:
- Detect sandbox vs app container via io.kubernetes.cri.container-type
  OCI annotation. Sandbox boots the VM; containers get block devices.
- Kubernetes sandbox-id grouping ensures pod containers share a shim.

Block device flow (host shim):
- Mount rootfs from containerd overlayfs snapshot
- Create ext4 disk image (sparse, sized to content + headroom)
- Hot-plug via Cloud Hypervisor vm.add-disk REST API
- Agent discovers disk, mounts it, runs crun

VM manager additions:
- add_disk() / remove_disk() via CH API
- api_request_to_socket() static method (avoids mutex across await)
- Parallel virtiofsd + CH startup (tokio::join!)
- 5ms socket polling, no 200ms sleep between create/boot
- 10ms agent health polling

Lifecycle:
- Sandbox signal marks all containers stopped + signals shim exit
- Wait checks local state first for instant return
- Drop uses SIGKILL + waitpid for reliable cleanup

commit.message:
feat(agent): crun runtime with OCI spec adaptation for VM environment

Replace runc with crun (1.5MB static vs 10MB) and adapt the OCI spec
for running containers inside a Cloud Hypervisor VM:

- OCI adaptation: strip host namespaces, hostname, AppArmor, seccomp,
  cgroup paths, resources, host bind mounts. Keep mount + PID namespaces
  for multi-container isolation within the VM.
- Device nodes: create /dev/null,zero,random,tty in container rootfs
  with proper permissions (umask(0) before mknod)
- Mount fixes: /tmp without MS_NODEV (device nodes must be openable)
- Block device discovery: scan /sys/block/ for hot-plugged vdX devices,
  mount ext4 at /run/containers/<id>
- Remove SIGCHLD handler (raced with tokio child reaper)
- Graceful lifecycle: handle already-exited containers in signal/wait,
  error chain display with {e:#}

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(shim): containerd shim v2 protocol integration

Fix critical issues preventing the shim from working with containerd:

- Remove stdout logging before run() — log output corrupted the TTRPC
  socket address that containerd reads from the shim's stdout
- Move logger init to create_task_service() (daemon mode only)
- Config path: /opt/cloudhv/config.json (matches installer)
- Fix ttrpc timeouts: with_timeout() takes nanoseconds, not seconds —
  30ns timeout caused instant RPC failures. Use with_duration() instead.
- Add oci-spec dependency for OCI annotation parsing
